popularStory.headline: David of Scotland, Earl of Huntingdon
popularStory.description:
David of Scotland (Medieval Gaelic: Dabíd) (c. 1144 – 17 June 1219) was a Scottish prince and Earl of Huntingdon. He was a claimant to the Scottish throne.

He was the youngest surviving son of Henr …

zh.lifeSummary: 當David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon於1144年出生在英國英格蘭亨廷格多史爾亨廷格頓時，他的父親Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria30歲，母親Ada de Warenne24歲。他和Matilda of Chester至少有2個兒子和4個女兒。他去世於1219年6月17日在蘇格蘭洛包爾德包享年75歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭坎布利其爾薩福崔。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon于1144年出生在英国英格兰亨廷格多史爾亨廷格頓时，他的父亲Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria30岁，母亲Ada de Warenne24岁。他和Matilda of Chester至少有2个儿子和4个女儿。他去世于1219年6月17日在苏格兰洛包爾德包享年75岁，埋葬在英国英格兰坎布利其爾薩福崔。

nameMeanings.name: 8th
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Swiss German: possibly from a short form of surnames formed with Id-, Ith-, the root of ancient Germanic origin, of disputed etymology, possibly cognate with Old Norse idh ‘work, activity’.
Cambodian: written អ៊ិត, unexplained.

nameMeanings.name: Earl
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: nickname from Middle English e(o)rl ‘earl’, a traditional English title of nobility equivalent in rank to French and Norman Count .

variants.name: Earle
meanings: English: variant of   Earl  .

variants.name: Harle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Dieter, Johann, Kurt.
South German (Härle): nickname from a diminutive of Middle High German hār ‘hair’.

English (northern) and Scottish: habitational name from Kirkharle and Little Harle in Northumberland (earlier simply Herle, Harle), possibly named from an Old English personal name Herela (a derivative of various compound names with the first element here ‘army’) + Old English lēah ‘wood, clearing’.

variants.name: Earls
meanings: English: variant of Earl with genitival -s, denoting someone who was a member of an earl's household, or, with post-medieval excrescent -s. In Ireland, when this is not the English name, it is from Gaelic Mac an Iarla ‘son of the earl’.

variants.name: Hearl
meanings: English: variant of Earl with prosthetic H-.

variants.name: Count
meanings: English: nickname from Anglo-Norman French counte ‘count’ (Old French conte, from Latin comes, comitem); a title of French and Norman nobility, ranking as equivalent to English Earl .

variants.name: Earll
meanings: English (Kent): variant of   Earl  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of Ō (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (오; Chinese character: 吳): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late Koryŏ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O Ch’ŏm (吳 瞻), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King Chijŭng (500–514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Huntingdon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: English:  see  Huntington  .

variants.name: Huntington
meanings: English (northern): habitational name from any of various places called Huntington, most of which are named with Old English huntena, the genitive plural form of hunta ‘hunter, huntsman’, + dūn ‘hill’ or tūn ‘farmstead, estate’. Some, however, may have Old English hunting ‘hunting’ as the initial element, while others may derive from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan), perhaps with Old English connective -ing-, + tūn. The surname may also arise from Huntingdon (Huntingdonshire), which derives from the Old English personal name Hunta (genitive Huntan) or Old English hunta (genitive huntan) + dūn.

History: A prominent American family of this name were founded by Simon Huntington, who himself never saw the New World, for he died in 1633 on the voyage to Boston, where his widow settled with her children. Their descendants include Jabez Huntington (1719–86), a wealthy West Indies trader, and Samuel Huntington (1731–96), who was one of the signers of the Declaration of Independence. Collis Potter Huntington (1821–1900) was an American railway magnate. Beginning with little education or money, he made a huge fortune, some of which he left to his nephew, Henry Huntington (1850–1927), who used the money to establish the Huntington library and art gallery in CA.

nameMeanings.name: David
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the greatest of all the kings of Israel, whose history is recounted with great vividness in the first and second books of Samuel and elsewhere. As a boy he killed the giant Philistine Goliath with his slingshot. As king of Judah, and later of all Israel, he expanded the power of the Israelites and established the security of their kingdom. He was also noted as a poet, many of the Psalms being attributed to him. The Hebrew derivation of the name is uncertain; it is said by some to represent a nursery word meaning ‘darling’. It is a very popular Jewish name, but is almost equally common among Gentiles in the English-speaking world. It is particularly common in Wales and Scotland, having been borne by the patron saint of Wales (  see  Dewi   ) and by two medieval kings of Scotland.

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of Oswald or any of the various other names beginning with Os-.

nameMeanings.name: Scotland
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Scottish (Kinross and Clackmannanshire):
from the Middle English personal name Scotland, Scolland. Found in Normandy as well as England, it evidently denoted a Scot or someone with an association with Scotland.
ethnic name for someone from Scotland, from Middle English Scotlond ‘Scotland’. Compare England .

variants.name: England
meanings: English: habitational name from Middle English Engelond ‘England’. It was probably a formal alternative to English , which is also well evidenced as a surname in England. These names may have been acquired by English landowners who moved in Norman social circles or who lived in a neighbouring country (Scotland, Wales, or Ireland), or by English merchants who traded abroad.
Norwegian: habitational name from any of various farmsteads so named, from Old Norse eng ‘meadow’ + land ‘land’.
Swedish: ornamental name with the same meaning as 2.

en.lifeSummary: When David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon was born in 1144, in Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria, was 30 and his mother, Ada de Warenne, was 24. He had at least 2 sons and 4 daughters with Matilda of Chester. He died on 17 June 1219, in Jedburgh, Roxburghshire, Scotland, at the age of 75, and was buried in Sawtry, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon est né en 1144, à Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Ada de Warenne, avait 24 ans. Il a eu au moins 2 fils et 4 filles avec Matilda of Chester. Il est décédé le 17 juin 1219, à Jedburgh, Roxburghshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 75 ans, et a été enterré à Sawtry, Cambridgeshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon 1144 in Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria 30 und seine Mutter Ada de Warenne 24. Er hatte mit Matilda of Chester mindestens 2 Söhne und 4 Töchter. Er starb am 17. Juni 1219 im Alter von 75 Jahren in Jedburgh, Roxburghshire, Scotland und wurde in Sawtry, Cambridgeshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon è nato il nel 1144 nel Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria, aveva 30 anni e il suo madre, Ada de Warenne, aveva 24 anni. Lui avuto almeno 2 figli e 4 figlie con Matilda of Chester. Morì il 17 giugno 1219, nel Jedburgh, Roxburghshire, Scotland, all'età di 75 anni, e fu sepolto a Sawtry, Cambridgeshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdonが1144年にイギリスイギリスハンティングドンシュアイアハンティングドンで生まれた時，父Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbriaは30歳，母Ada de Warenneは24歳であった。彼はMatilda of Chesterとの間に少なくとも2人の息子と4人の娘をもうけた。彼は1219年6月17日にスコットランドロクスブルグhシュアイアジェドブルグで75歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスカンブリジェシュアイアサウトリーにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon(이)가 1144년 영국 잉글랜드 헌틴그도실르 헌틴그던에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria(은)는 30세였고, 그의 어머니 Ada de Warenne(은)는 24세였다. 그는 Matilda of Chester(와)과 적어도 두 명의 아들과 네 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1219년 6월 17일 스컷랜드 록스버그h실르 제드버그에서 75세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 캄브리지실르 사w트리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon nasceu em 1144, em Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria, tinha 30 anos e sua mãe, Ada de Warenne, tinha 24 anos. Ele teve pelo menos 2 filhos e 4 filhas com Matilda of Chester. Ele faleceu em 17 de junho de 1219, em Jedburgh, Roxburghshire, Scotland, com 75 anos, e foi sepultado em Sawtry, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon родился в 1144 г. в местности хантингдон, хантингдоншир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria было 30 лет, а матери, которую звали Ada de Warenne, 24. У него было по крайней мере 2 сына и 4 дочери от Matilda of Chester. Он умер 17 июня 1219 г. в местности джедбург, роксбургшир, Шотландия в возрасте 75 лет и был похоронен в местности савтри, камбриджешир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando David of Scotland 8th Earl of Huntingdon nació en 1144, en Huntingdon, Huntingdonshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Henry of Scotland Earl of Huntingdon and Northumbria, tenía 30 años y su mamá, Ada de Warenne, tenía 24 años. Tuvo por lo menos 2 hijos y 4 hijas con Matilda of Chester. Murió el 17 de junio de 1219, en Jedburgh, Roxburghshire, Scotland, a la edad de 75 años, y fue sepultado en Sawtry, Cambridgeshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
